problem text: At a pond, you catch $50$ fish and mark them with tags. On the following day, you catch another $40$ fish and find that $10$ of them are tagged. Estimate the total number of fish in the pond.
problem solution: $ \frac{1}{4} $ of the fish caught had tags, suggesting that approximately $ \frac{1}{4} $ of the entire fish population is tagged. Consequently, there are an estimated $ 50 \cdot 4 = 200 $ fish in the pond.
problem hint: $\dfrac{1}{4}$ of the fish caught had tags.
